item.title: How location sharing helped police catch a serial rapist-turned-killer
item.description: The mother of murdered model Christy Giles pleads for others to share their locations. She says the technology helped police catch David Pearce, who murdered Giles and her friend, architect Hilda Marcela Cabrales.

item.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
item.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

item.title: "Dead Girls Don't Talk"
item.description: A man charged with murder for poisoning two women tells a friend, "dead girls don't talk." Brave survivors speak out on their behalf. "48 Hours" contributor Jonathan Vigliotti reports.

item.title: Newsom declares state of emergency in Los Angeles for Boyle Heights warehouse fire
item.description: Firefighters faced renewed challenges Saturday at a large Boyle Heights warehouse fire, where conditions remain highly complex as Mayor Karen Bass declared a local emergency to support response efforts.

item.title: Timeline: Investigating the Long Island serial killings
item.description: What began as a search for one missing woman — Shannan Gilbert — led to multiple bodies and the capture of Rex Heuermann.

item.title: Vance heads to Switzerland for talks; Iran says it's closing Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran's military accused the U.S. of not upholding the first point in the memorandum of understanding, with Israel remaining in Lebanese territory.

item.title: Small plane crashes into lake in West Milford, New Jersey
item.description: A small plane crashed into a lake in West Milford, New Jersey, on Saturday.

item.title: Survivors speak up to help convict man of murder, sexual assault
item.description: David Pearce was convicted of first-degree murder for the deaths of Christy Giles and Hilda Marcela Cabrales after a night of partying in Los Angeles. He was also found guilty of raping seven other women who came forward to testify at his trial.

item.title: Trump says several people arrested for alleged vandalism of Reflecting Pool
item.description: President Trump claims the problems with the Reflecting Pool in Washington are due to vandalism.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: 6/20: CBS Weekend News
item.description: Vice President JD Vance heads to Switzerland for peace talks with Iran; President Trump defends his beautification push in Washington, D.C.

item.title: Company owned by Trump donor won $1.7 million no-bid Reflecting Pool contract
item.description: The federal government awarded a company owned by a Trump donor $1.7 million to install a new water cleaning system for the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, records show.

item.title: Historic D.C. castle reopens after extensive renovation
item.description: The historic Smithsonian Castle in Washington, D.C., which closed for a multi-year renovation, has temporarily reopened to the public for America's 250th anniversary. Natalie Brand has more.

item.title: Democratic socialism in the midst of a U.S. revival
item.description: Democratic socialism appears to be on the rise in the U.S. The progressive ideology gained prominence when New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani took office. Now, more candidates seem to be gaining momentum in major U.S. cities. Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Trump defends beautification push in D.C.
item.description: President Trump's push to renovate landmarks in Washington, D.C., ahead of America's 250th birthday is drawing criticism from critics who say the process is being conducted without the usual approvals. Taurean Small explains.

item.title: Despite dip in gas prices, many still feeling economic pinch going into summer travel season
item.description: U.S. drivers are feeling less pain at the pump than in recent months. But for many, it's tough to feel a change. Max Darrow has more.

item.title: Vance heads to Switzerland for Iran peace talks
item.description: Vice President JD Vance departed for Switzerland Saturday to begin direct long-term peace negotiations with Iran. His trip comes as shipping in the Strait of Hormuz faced new disruptions after Iran said it was closing the waterway. Imtiaz Tyab reports.

item.title: Dangerous weather threatens multiple states
item.description: Severe weather impacted several regions across the U.S. Minnesota got a preview Friday night when hail pummeled the Twin Cities. On Saturday, thunderstorms and potential tornadoes threatened parts of the South. CBS Philadelphia meteorologist Tammie Souza has the latest.

item.title: Zelenskyy returns Polish honor after president revokes it in history dispute
item.description: Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy has returned a state honor to Poland after the Polish president revoked it.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (June 21)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: 3 elderly hikers die on Grand Canyon's inner trails as temperatures spike
item.description: Emergency responders arrived to find the hikers already deceased on the trails, according to the National Park Service.

item.title: Claude Guillemot, co-founder of Ubisoft, dies in plane crash
item.description: Claude Guillemot and a flight instructor were flying in a twin-motor Cessna 421 on Friday evening. An investigation into the crash is underway.

item.title: Tay Keith, Grammy-nominated producer, found dead in Nashville at 29
item.description: Record producer Tay Keith was found dead in his Nashville home by officers performing a welfare check, police said.

item.title: Rips appear in Reflecting Pool's new sealant after algae bloom turn water green
item.description: President Trump's efforts to spruce up the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool faced a snag this week, with algae turning the water green while rips appeared in an "American Flag Blue" surface picked by the president.

item.title: Trump unveils new Air Force One, a $400 million plane gifted by Qatar
item.description: "This is considered the world's most luxurious plane," the president said in front of the enormous new jet.

item.title: George W. Bush gifts Michelle Obama mints in an ode to their friendship
item.description: The Altoids were a callback to a viral moment between former first lady Michelle Obama and former President George W. Bush.

item.title: What's missing from the Epstein files? Here's what we found
item.description: The Justice Department says it's released "every document required by the Epstein Files Transparency Act," but CBS News has identified numerous gaps.

item.title: Scientists discover parasite that preys on insect-killing "zombie fungus"
item.description: Scientists say the "hyperparasite" preys on "zombie fungus" known to infect insects before subjecting them to a gruesome death.

item.title: NYC celebrates Knicks NBA championship with ticker-tape parade
item.description: The New York Knicks went up New York City's famous Canyon of Heroes for a massive ticker-tape parade celebrating the 2026 NBA champions.

item.title: Less than half of Americans can afford quality healthcare, Gallup finds
item.description: Gallup found that only 49% of Americans were "cost-secure" last year, with concerns about medical bills and prescription costs rising across income groups.
